name: sinol-make

metadata.name: sinol-make
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: CLI tool for creating sio2 task packages

documentation:
# ![Logo](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2264918?s=60&v=4) sinol-make `sinol-make` is a CLI tool for creating and verifying problem packages for [sio2](https://github.com/sio2project/oioioi) with features such as: - measuring time and memory in the same deterministic way as sio2, - running the solutions in parallel, - keeping a git-friendly report of solutions' scores, - catching mistakes in the problem packages as early as possible, - and more. ## Contents - [Why?](#why) - [Installation](#installation) - [Usage](#usage) - [Contest types](#contest-types) - [Reporting bugs and contributing code](#reporting-bugs-and-contributing-code) ### Why? The purpose of the tool is to make it easier to create good problem packages for official competitions, which requires collaboration with other people and using a multitude of "good practices" recommendations. While there are several excellent CLI tools for creating tests and solutions, they lack some built-in mechanisms for verifying packages and finding mistakes before uploading the package to the judge system. As sinol-make was created specifically for the sio2 problem packages, by default it downloads and uses sio2's deterministic mechanism of measuring solutions' runtime, called `sio2jail`. ### Installation It's possible to directly install [sinol-make](https://pypi.org/project/sinol-make/) through Python's package manager pip, which usually is installed alongside Python: ```bash pip3 install sinol-make ``` `pip` installs the `sinol-make` executable in `~/.local/bin/` directory, so make sure this directory is in your `PATH`. [Here's](https://gist.github.com/nex3/c395b2f8fd4b02068be37c961301caa7) how to add a directory to `PATH`. As `sio2jail` works only on Linux-based operating systems, *we do not recommend* using operating systems such as Windows or macOS. Nevertheless `sinol-make` supports those operating systems, though there are additional installation steps required to use other tools for measuring time (which are non-deterministic and produce reports different from sio2): - Debian-based systems (Ubuntu, usually Windows WSL): `apt install time` - Arch-based systems: `pacman -S time` - macOS: `brew install gnu-time coreutils` #### Autocompletion (optional) If you would like to have autocompletion for `sinol-make` commands, run the following command and refresh the shell (e.g. by opening a new terminal): ```shell activate-global-python-argcomplete ``` ### Usage The available commands (see `sinol-make --help`) are: - `sinol-make run` -- Runs selected solutions (by default all solutions) on selected tests (by default all tests) with a given number of CPUs. Measures the solutions' time with sio2jail, unless specified otherwise. After running the solutions, it compares the solutions' scores with the ones saved in config.yml. If you're using sio2jail, make sure you are not running on efficiency cpu cores. You can check if you have them [like this](https://stackoverflow.com/a/71282744). To run on normal cpu cores, use `taskset -c 8-15 sinol-make ...`, assuming that cpu cores 8-15 are not efficiency cores. Run `sinol-make run --help` to see available flags. - `sinol-make gen` -- Generate input files using ingen program (for example prog/abcingen.cpp for abc task). Whenever the new input differs from the previous one, the model solution will be used to generate the new output file. You can also specify your ingen source file which will be used. Run `sinol-make gen --help` to see available flags. - `sinol-make ingen` -- Generate input files using ingen program (for example prog/abcingen.cpp for abc task). You can also specify your ingen source file which will be used. Run `sinol-make ingen --help` to see available flags. - `sinol-make outgen` -- Generate output files using the model solutions. Run `sinol-make outgen --help` to see available flags. - `sinol-make inwer` -- Verifies whether input files are correct using your "inwer.cpp" program. You can specify what inwer program to use, what tests to check and how many CPUs to use. Run `sinol-make inwer --help` to see available flags. - `sinol-make export` -- Creates archive ready to upload to sio2 or szkopul. Run `sinol-make export --help` to see all available flags. - `sinol-make doc` -- Compiles all LaTeX files in doc/ directory to PDF. Run `sinol-make doc --help` to see all available flags. - `sinol-make verify` -- Verifies the package. This command runs stress tests (if available), verifies the config, generates tests, generates problem statements, runs inwer and run all solutions. Ingen and inwer are compiled with address and UB sanitizers. Run `sinol-make verify --help` to see all available flags. - `sinol-make chkwer` -- Run checker with model solution and print results. Prints a table with points and checker's comments. This command fails if the model solution didn't score maximum points. Run `sinol-make chkwer --help` to see all available flags. - `sinol-make init [id]` -- Creates package from template [on github](https://github.com/sio2project/sinol-make/tree/main/example_package) and sets task id to provided `[id]`. Requires an internet connection to run. You can also run multiple commands at once, for example: ```shell sinol-make gen prog/abcingen2.cpp inwer --cpus 4 run --tests abc1*.in doc export --no-statement ``` There are also available short aliases for the commands: - `sinol-make r` for `sinol-make run` - `sinol-make g` for `sinol-make gen` - `sinol-make i` for `sinol-make inwer` - `sinol-make e` for `sinol-make export` - `sinol-make d` for `sinol-make doc` - `sinol-make v` for `sinol-make verify` - `sinol-make c` for `sinol-make chkwer` - `sm` for `sinol-make` ### Contest types `sinol-make` changes its behavior depending on the contest type specified in `config.yml`. You can specify the contest type with the `sinol_contest_type` key in config. Here is the table of available contest types and their features: | Feature | `default` | `oi` | `oij` | `icpc` | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|----------|----------|--------| | Max score | 100 | 100 | 100 | 1 | | Default time tool | sio2jail | sio2jail | sio2jail | time | | Full score if took less than half of the time limit,
otherwise linearly decreasing to 1. | ❌ | ✔️ | ❌ | ❌ | | Full score if took less than the time limit | ✔️ | ❌ | ✔️ | ✔️ | | Scores must sum up to 100 | ❌ | ✔️ | ✔️ | ❌ | | Limits can be set for individual tests | ✔️ | ❌ | ✔️ | ✔️ | | Verifies if tests are named correctly
(letters within groups increase, group numbers increase) | ❌ | ✔️ | ✔️ | ✔️ | ### Reporting bugs and contributing code - Want to report a bug or request a feature? [Open an issue](https://github.com/sio2project/sinol-make/issues). - Want to help us build `sinol-make`? Create a Pull Request and we will gladly review it.

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType.identify
documentation:
Returns a tuple of two values:
- bool: whether the task is of this type
- int: priority of this task type

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType.name
documentation: Returns the name of task type.

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType._check_task_type_changed
documentation: Checks if task type has changed and if so, deletes cache.

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType._check_had_file
documentation:
Checks if there was a file (e.g. checker) and if it is now removed (or the other way around) and if so,
removes tests cache.

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType.additional_files_to_compile
documentation:
Returns a list of tuples of two values:
- str: source file path to compile
- str: path to the compiled file
- str: name of the compiled file
- bool: whether the program should fail on compilation error
- bool: whether all cache should be cleared on file change

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType.run_outgen
documentation: Whether outgen should be run.

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType.allow_chkwer
documentation: Whether chkwer should be allowed to run.

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.BaseTaskType.check_output
documentation:
Runs the checker (or runs diff) and returns a tuple of three values:
- bool: whether the solution is correct
- Fraction: percentage of the score
- str: optional comment

functions.name: sinol_make.sio2jail.install_sio2jail

functions.name: sinol_make.sio2jail.check_perf_counters_enabled
documentation: Checks if sio2jail is able to use perf counters to count instructions.

classes.name: sinol_make.contest_types.icpc.ICPCContest
documentation:
Contest type for ACM ICPC type contest.
The possible score for one solution is 1 or 0.
The score is 0 if any of the tests fail.

Full score if took less than half of limit, otherwise linearly decreasing to 1.
This function is copied from `oioioi` code.
https://github.com/sio2project/oioioi/blob/40a377d3f2d5cd9c94d01f03e197501ce4aab597/oioioi/programs/utils.py#L107

methods.name: sinol_make.task_type.interactive.InteractiveTaskType.Pipes.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for Pipes class.
\:param r_interactor: file descriptor from which the interactor reads from the solution
\:param w_interactor: file descriptor to which the interactor writes to the solution
\:param r_solution: file descriptor from which the solution reads from the interactor
\:param w_solution: file descriptor to which the solution writes to the interactor
Class for storing file descriptors for interactor and solution processes.

classes.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.get_type
documentation: Returns type of contest.

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.argument_overrides
documentation: Add contest specific arguments

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.assign_scores
documentation:
Returns dictionary with scores for each group.
Called if `scores` is not specified in config.
\:param groups: List of groups
\:return: Dictionary: {"\<group>": \<points>}

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.get_possible_score
documentation:
Get the maximum possible score.
\:param groups: List of groups.
\:param scores: Dictionary: {"\<group>": \<points>}
\:return: Maximum possible score.

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.get_test_score
documentation:
Returns points for test.
\:param result: result of execution
\:param time_limit: time limit for test
\:param memory_limit: memory limit for test
\:return: points for test

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.get_group_score
documentation:
Calculates group score based on tests scores.
\:param test_scores: List of scores for tests
\:param group_max_score: Maximum score for group
\:return:

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.get_global_score
documentation:
Calculates global score based on groups scores.
\:param groups_scores: Dictionary: {"\<group>: {"status": Status, "points": \<points for group>}
\:param global_max_score: Maximum score for contest
\:return: Global score

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.verify_config
documentation: Used for verifing contest specific config.yml settings

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.additional_export_job
documentation:
Called once during package export, just before compressing it to archive.
Allows for contest specific jobs before during package export.
\:return: If not None, returned value will be used as name of the archive

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.min_score_per_test
documentation: Returns minimum score for single test

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.max_score_per_test
documentation: Returns maximum score for single test

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.verify_pre_gen
documentation: Called by verify command before generating tests.

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.allow_per_test_limits
documentation: Returns True if limits in config can be set per test

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.preferred_timetool
documentation: Returns preferred time tool

methods.name: sinol_make.contest_types.default.DefaultContest.verify_tests_order
documentation: Whether to verify tests order

Default contest type.
Points for tests are equal to points from execution result.
Group score is equal to minimum score from tests.
Global score is sum of group scores.
Scores for groups are assigned equally.
Max possible score is sum of group scores.

classes.name: sinol_make.executors.BaseExecutor

methods.name: sinol_make.executors.BaseExecutor._wrap_command
documentation: Wraps the command with the necessary tools to measure time and memory usage.

methods.name: sinol_make.executors.BaseExecutor._execute
documentation:
This function should run subprocess.Popen with the given command and return a tuple of three values:
- bool: whether the process was terminated due to time limit
- bool: whether the process was terminated due to memory limit
- int: return code of the process
- List[str]: stderr of the process

methods.name: sinol_make.executors.BaseExecutor._parse_result
documentation: Parses the result file and returns the result.

methods.name: sinol_make.executors.BaseExecutor.execute
documentation: Executes the command and returns the result, stdout and stderr.
Base class for executors. Executors are used to run commands and measure their time and memory usage.

classes.name: sinol_make.executors.detailed.DetailedExecutor
documentation: Executor which doesn't use time or sio2jail for measuring time and memory usage.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.get_commands
documentation: Function to get an array of all available commands.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.get_command_names
documentation: Function to get an array of all available command names.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.find_and_chdir_package
documentation:
Checks if current directory or parent directory is a package directory.
If it is, it changes the current working directory to it and returns True.
If it isn't, it returns False.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.init_package_command
documentation:
Updates arguments with contest specific overrides for commands
that require being in package directory

functions.name: sinol_make.util.exit_if_not_package
documentation:
Checks if current directory or parent directory is a package directory.
If it is, current working directory is changed to it.
If it isn't, it exits with an error.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.save_config
documentation: Function to save nicely formated config.yml.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.import_importlib_resources
documentation:
Function to import importlib_resources.
For Python 3.8 and below, we use importlib_resources.
For Python 3.9 and above, we use importlib.resources.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.is_dev
documentation: Function to check if the version is a development version.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.check_for_updates
documentation:
Function to check if there is a new version of sinol-make.
\:param current_version: current version of sinol-make
\:param check: whether to check for new version
\:return: returns new version if there is one, None otherwise

functions.name: sinol_make.util.check_version
documentation:
Function that asynchronously checks for new version of sinol-make.
Writes the newest version to data/version file.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.lines_diff
documentation:
Function to compare two lists of lines.
Returns True if they are the same, False otherwise.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.file_diff
documentation:
Function to compare two files.
Returns True if they are the same, False otherwise.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.get_terminal_size
documentation:
Function to get the size of the terminal.
\:return: triple (has_terminal, width, height)

functions.name: sinol_make.util.get_templates_dir
documentation:
Function to get the path to the templates' directory.
\:return: path to the templates directory

functions.name: sinol_make.util.stringify_keys
documentation: Function to stringify all keys in a dict.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.change_stack_size_to_unlimited
documentation: Function to change the stack size to unlimited.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.is_wsl
documentation: Function to check if the program is running on Windows Subsystem for Linux.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.is_linux
documentation: Function to check if the program is running on Linux (including WSL).

functions.name: sinol_make.util.is_macos
documentation: Function to check if the program is running on macOS.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.is_macos_arm
documentation: Function to check if the program is running on macOS on ARM.

functions.name: sinol_make.util.try_fix_config
documentation:
Function to try to fix the config.yml file.
Tries to:
\- reformat `sinol_expected_scores` field
\:param config: config.yml file as a dict
\:return: config.yml file as a dict

functions.name: sinol_make.util.default_cpu_count
documentation: Function to get default number of cpus to use for multiprocessing.

classes.name: sinol_make.commands.ingen.Command
documentation: Class for `ingen` command.

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.outgen.outgen_util.get_correct_solution
documentation:
Returns path to correct solution for given task.
\:param task_id: task id, for example abc
\:return: path to correct solution or None if not found

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.outgen.outgen_util.compile_correct_solution
documentation: Compiles correct solution and returns path to compiled executable.

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.outgen.outgen_util.generate_output
documentation:
Generates output file for given input file.
\:param arguments: arguments for output generation (type OutputGenerationArguments)
\:return: True if the output was successfully generated, False otherwise

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.ingen.ingen_util.ingen_exists
documentation:
Checks if ingen source file exists.
\:param task_id: task id, for example abc
\:return: True if exists, False otherwise

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.ingen.ingen_util.get_ingen
documentation:
Find ingen source file in `prog/` directory.
If `ingen_path` is specified, then it will be used (if exists).
\:param task_id: task id, for example abc.
\:param ingen_path: path to ingen source file
\:return: path to ingen source file or None if not found

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.ingen.ingen_util.compile_ingen
documentation:
Compiles ingen and returns path to compiled executable.
If ingen_path is shell script, then it will be returned.

functions.name: sinol_make.commands.ingen.ingen_util.run_ingen
documentation:
Runs ingen and generates all input files.
\:param ingen_exe: path to ingen executable
\:param working_dir: working directory for ingen. If None, then {os.getcwd()}/in is used.
\:return: True if ingen was successful, False otherwise

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compile.compile
documentation:
Compile a program.
\:param program: Path to the program to compile
\:param output: Path to the output file
\:param compilers: Compilers object
\:param compile_log: File to write the compilation log to
\:param compilation_flags: Group of compilation flags to use
\:param extra_compilation_args: Extra compilation arguments
\:param extra_compilation_files: Extra compilation files
\:param clear_cache: Set to True if you want to delete all cached test results.
\:param use_fsanitize: Whether to use fsanitize when compiling C/C++ programs. Sanitizes address and undefined behavior.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compile.compile_file
documentation:
Compile a file
\:param file_path: Path to the file to compile
\:param name: Name of the executable
\:param compilers: Compilers object
\:param compilation_flags: Group of compilation flags to use
\:param use_fsanitize: Whether to use fsanitize when compiling C/C++ programs. Sanitizes address and undefined behavior.
\:param additional_flags: Additional flags for c / c++ compiler.
\:param use_extras: Whether to use extra compilation files and arguments from config
\:return: Tuple of (executable path or None if compilation failed, log path)

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compile.print_compile_log
documentation:
Print the first 500 lines of compilation log
\:param compile_log_path: path to the compilation log

classes.name: sinol_make.helpers.classinit.RegisteredSubclassesBase
documentation:
A base class for classes which should have a list of subclasses
available.

The list of subclasses is available in their {attr}`subclasses` class
attributes. Classes which have *explicitly* set {attr}`abstract` class
attribute to `True` are not added to {attr}`subclasses`.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compiler.get_python_interpreter_path
documentation: Get the Python interpreter

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compiler.get_java_compiler_path
documentation: Get the Java compiler

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compiler.get_default_compilers
documentation: Get the default compilers

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.compiler.verify_compilers
documentation:
Verify that specified compilers exist.
\:param args: argparse.Namespace arguments
\:param solutions: List of solutions (used for checking if a compiler is specified)
\:return: Compilers object with all the compilers

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.func_cache.cache_result
documentation: Function to cache the result of a function.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.func_cache.clear_cache
documentation: Function to clear the cache.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.printer.printer
documentation:
Prints output of func (called every 0.1 seconds with terminal width and height, then args and kwargs)
to terminal in less-style.
\:param func: function called to get output. Should return a triple (output, title, footer),

> where output is a list of lines to print,
> title is a string printed at the top of the terminal,
> and footer is a string printed at the bottom of the terminal.
> Title and footer can be None.

```
param args

args for func

param kwargs

kwargs for func
```

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.printer.printer_thread
documentation:
Same as printer, but with threading.Event to stop printing.
\:param run_event: threading.Event that is set when printer should stop
\:param func: function called to get output. Should return a triple (output, title, footer),

> where output is a list of lines to print,
> title is a string printed at the top of the terminal,
> and footer is a string printed at the bottom of the terminal.
> Title and footer can be None.

```
param args

args for func

param kwargs

kwargs for func
```

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.printer._printer
documentation:
Function called by curses.wrapper to print output of func (called with terminal width and height, then args and kwargs) to terminal in less-style.
\:param func: function called to get output. Should return a triple (output, title, footer),

> where output is a list of lines to print,
> title is a string printed at the top of the terminal,
> and footer is a string printed at the bottom of the terminal.
> Title and footer can be None.

```
param args

args for func

param kwargs

kwargs for func
```

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.printer._print_to_scr
documentation: Prints output to scr. Replaces color escape sequences with curses color escape sequences.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.paths.get_cache_path
documentation:
Function to get a path in the cache directory. Works the same as os.path.join.
With no arguments, it returns the path to the cache directory.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.paths.get_executables_path
documentation:
Function to get a path in executables directory. Works the same as os.path.join.
With no arguments, it returns the path to the executables directory.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.paths.get_compilation_log_path
documentation:
Function to get a path in compilation log directory. Works the same as os.path.join.
With no arguments, it returns the path to the compilation log directory.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.paths.get_executions_path
documentation:
Function to get a path in executions directory. Works the same as os.path.join.
With no arguments, it returns the path to the executions directory.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.paths.get_chkwer_path
documentation:
Function to get a path in chkwer directory. In this directory chkwer will generate outputs.
Works the same as os.path.join. With no arguments, it returns the path to the chkwer directory.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.get_cache_file
documentation:
Returns content of cache file for given solution
\:param solution_path: Path to solution
\:return: Content of cache file

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.check_compiled
documentation:
Check if a file is compiled
\:param file_path: Path to the file
\:return: executable path if compiled, None otherwise

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.save_compiled
documentation:
Save the compiled executable path to cache in `.cache/md5sums/<basename of file_path>`,
which contains the md5sum of the file and the path to the executable.
\:param file_path: Path to the file
\:param exe_path: Path to the compiled executable
\:param compilation_flags: Compilation flags used
\:param sanitizers: Whether -fsanitize=undefined,address was used
\:param clear_cache: Set to True if you want to delete all cached test results.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.process_extra_compilation_files
documentation:
Checks if extra compilation files have changed and saves them to cache.
If they have, removes all cached solutions that use them.
\:param extra_compilation_files: List of extra compilation files
\:param task_id: Task id

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.process_extra_execution_files
documentation:
Checks if extra execution files have changed and saves them to cache.
If they have, removes all cached solutions that use them.
\:param extra_execution_files: List of extra execution files
\:param task_id: Task id

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.remove_results_cache
documentation: Removes all cached test results

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.remove_results_if_contest_type_changed
documentation:
Checks if contest type has changed and removes all cached test results if it has.
\:param contest_type: Contest type

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.check_can_access_cache
documentation: Checks if user can access cache.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.has_file_changed
documentation:
Checks if file has changed since last compilation.
\:param file_path: Path to the file
\:return: True if file has changed, False otherwise

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.check_correct_solution
documentation:
Checks if correct solution has changed. If it did, removes cache for input files.
\:param task_id: Task id

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.cache.create_cache_dirs
documentation: Creates all required cache directories.

modules.name: sinol_make.commands.run
documentation: Modified version of https://sinol3.dasie.mimuw.edu.pl/oij/jury/package/-/blob/master/runner.py
Author of the original code: Bartosz Kostka <kostka@oij.edu.pl>

classes.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.run_solution
documentation: Run an execution and return the result as ExecutionResult object.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.run_solutions
documentation: Run solutions on tests and print the results as a table to stdout.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.convert_status_to_string
documentation: Converts all `Status` enums in dict to strings.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.get_whole_groups
documentation: Returns a list of groups for which all tests were run.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.get_valid_input_files
documentation: Returns list of input files that have corresponding output file.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.validate_existence_of_outputs
documentation: Checks if all input files have corresponding output files.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.check_are_any_tests_to_run
documentation:
Checks if there are any tests to run and prints them and checks
if all input files have corresponding output files.

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.check_errors
documentation:
Checks if there were any errors during execution and exits if there were.
\:param results: Dictionary of results.
\:return:

methods.name: sinol_make.commands.run.Command.print_checker_comments
documentation: Prints checker's comments for all tests and solutions.
Class for running current task

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.extract_test_id
documentation:
Extracts test group and number from test path.
For example for test abc1a.in it returns 1a.
\:param test_path: Path to test file.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:return: Test group and number.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_solutions_re
documentation:
Returns regex pattern matching all solutions for given task.
\:param task_id: Task id.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_files_matching
documentation:
Returns list of files matching given patterns.
If pattern is absolute path, it is returned as is.
If pattern is relative path, it is searched in current directory and in directory specified as argument.
\:param patterns: List of patterns to match.
\:param directory: Directory to search in.
\:return: List of files matching given patterns.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_tests
documentation:
Returns list of tests to run.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:param arg_tests: Tests specified in command line arguments. If None, all tests are returned.
\:return: List of tests to run.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_solutions
documentation:
Returns list of solutions to run.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:param args_solutions: Solutions specified in command line arguments. If None, all solutions are returned.
\:return: List of solutions to run.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_correct_solution
documentation:
Returns path to correct solution.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:return: Path to correct solution.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_executable_path
documentation: Returns path to compiled executable for given solution.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_time_limit
documentation: Returns time limit for given test.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_memory_limit
documentation: Returns memory limit for given test.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.validate_test_names
documentation: Checks if all files in the package have valid names.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_all_code_files
documentation:
Returns all code files in package.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:return: List of code files.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_files_matching_pattern
documentation:
Returns all files in package matching given pattern.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:param pattern: Pattern to match.
\:return: List of files matching the pattern.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.any_files_matching_pattern
documentation:
Returns True if any file in package matches given pattern.
\:param task_id: Task id.
\:param pattern: Pattern to match.
\:return: True if any file in package matches given pattern.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.check_if_contest_type_changed
documentation:
Checks if contest type in cache is different then contest type specified in config.yml.
\:param contest_type: Contest type specified in config.yml.
\:return: True if contest type in cache is different then contest type specified in config.yml.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.save_contest_type_to_cache
documentation:
Saves contest type to cache.
\:param contest_type: Contest type.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.validate_test
documentation:
Check if test doesn't contain leading/trailing whitespaces,
has only one space between tokens and ends with newline.
Exits with error if any of the conditions is not met.
\:return: Tuple of two values: True if test is valid, error message otherwise.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.validate_tests
documentation: Validate all tests in parallel.

functions.name: sinol_make.helpers.package_util.get_out_from_in
documentation: Returns path to output file corresponding to given input file.
